article_meta.title: | Updated November 25, 2025 9:00am EST Nearly 1 in 4 American households

reasoning: The summary is largely neutral, cites the report, and avoids loaded rhetoric. It echoes a headline phrase (“hit hardest”) and presents “lifestyle creep” and “K-shaped economy” without explicit attribution in one key fact, but overall does not adopt sensational framing or subjective judgments.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral phrasing; minor echo of ‘hit hardest’ but otherwise objective.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Figures and trends are attributed to the Bank of America Institute; minor lack of explicit attribution on ‘lifestyle creep’/‘K-shaped economy’.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Slightly states causes (lifestyle creep) as fact without ‘according to’, but grounded in the report.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Single-source report; summary reflects it proportionally without favoring an unrepresented side.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes the key perspectives present (BofA Institute, economist quotes).
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal; ‘hit hardest’ is mild and data-supported.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focus matches the article’s emphasis on financial strain; no added negativity.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Relies on the report as the article does; no undue deference beyond source content.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Overall neutral; does not replicate any loaded or partisan framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures the primary source and quoted economist; no major perspective omitted.
